title: Review-required routing
implementation_summary: Added the control-plane Claude review Review-required routing slice for RP00.P02.M04.S14.

The S14 policy, result-field registry, prechecker module, service function, validator, synthetic fixture, contract definition, README, and RP00 validator bind a frozen metadata-only review-required routing receipt after RP00.P02.M04.S13.

The precheck consumes and revalidates the S13 Validation error mapping result, requires the carried claude_review entrypoint, claude-opus-4-8 model, max effort, read-only metadata, validation error mapping receipt, audit event ref, allow_metadata_only validation error mapping decision, and applied validation error mapping marker.

The allowed path emits a frozen review_required_metadata_only receipt and result without invoking Claude at runtime, writing review queues, assigning reviewers, notifying reviewers, writing human approval records, writing databases, writing storage, persisting product state, writing persistence records, appending audit events, writing permission state, writing idempotency or lock stores, creating UI, executing service logic, creating runtime routes, or using real data.

Unsafe or unknown review-required routing claims fail closed, including runtime Claude invocation, review queue, assignment, notification, human approval, database, storage, product-state persistence, audit, permission, idempotency, lock, persistence, error, UI, bypass, real-data, and source-completion claims.

The dedicated service tests prove policy binding, metadata-only review-required receipt emission, firm-level metadata acceptance, result drift rejection, unsafe-claim and wrong-source failures, prechecker no-write boundaries, terminal M04 closeout, M05.S01 handoff, and synthetic fixture parity.
Claude review returned PASS/APPROVE with no P0/P1/P2 findings. The single P3 stale S15 failure-message finding was fixed before production_ready.
RP00.P02.M04 is completed by S14 and hands off to RP00.P02.M05.S01 Permission And Audit Binding; RP00.P02 and RP00 remain open.
